name: Explorer
description: Spawn read-only explorer sub-agents to investigate any directory or codebase — even outside your own workspace. Each Explore() call runs an independent explorer (its own model, prompt, and tools, configured centrally) that greps/reads the target path with the DirExplorer worker tools and reports findings back to you via agent messaging. Requires the DirExplorer skill to be enabled.
brief: You can investigate any directory or codebase — even outside your own workspace — with the `explorer` skill. Load it with `use_skill('explorer')`, then call `Explore({path, context_vars})` to spawn a read-only explorer sub-agent that searches the target path and reports its findings back to you automatically. Call it multiple times to explore several paths in parallel.
author: Evonic

variables.name: model_id
variables.label: Explorer model
variables.description: The LLM model explorer sub-agents use. Leave on the global default to inherit the platform default model.

variables.name: fallback_model_id
variables.label: Explorer fallback model
variables.description: Model used if the primary explorer model fails. Leave as 'No fallback' to disable.

variables.name: system_prompt
variables.label: Explorer system prompt
variables.default: You are an Explorer sub-agent: a focused, read-only code & directory investigator. You have been given a target directory to explore using the Grep, Glob, and Read tools (all paths resolve against that directory). Investigate thoroughly and efficiently, then report a clear, concise summary of your findings back to whoever delegated the task. Do NOT make a plan or ask for approval — explore directly until you have an answer. You cannot modify any files.
variables.description: System prompt for explorer sub-agents. Supports {{variable}} placeholders filled from the context_vars passed to Explore().

variables.name: tool_ids
variables.label: Extra explorer tools
variables.description: Comma-separated tool IDs to grant explorers IN ADDITION to the mandatory DirExplorer Grep/Read/Glob. Each must be a valid, enabled tool (e.g. skill:someskill:fn). Invalid or disabled references cause Explore() to error.
